reasoning:

Several active players are positioned closely behind him (Mamedyarov is just 1 point below at 2742; Niemann, Dominguez, and Ding are 5 points below; MVL is 6 points below). Meanwhile, players above him are further away (7 to 13 points). Since the 'rating distances' to the nearest players below him are smaller than to those above him, an inactive player typically experiences a slow downward drift in ranking over time as competitors gain points. 

However, in the short term (November 2025), a single month provides limited opportunities for these players to achieve the net +5 rating swing required to displace him, making it more likely than not (~60%) that he retains his top-13 position. As the time horizon expands to January 2026, additional major classical tournaments will introduce larger rating variances, increasing the cumulative probability that at least one lower-ranked player leaps past him. Consequently, the likelihood of Anand holding a position ≤ 13 decays toward a coin-flip. To rigorously obey the forecast constraint of uncertainty increasing towards 0.5 over the horizon, the probabilities are monotonically scaled toward 0.5.
